title: ServiceAccount mountable-secrets restriction bypass via envFrom secretRef
category: missing-check-arm (authorization bypass: one of several secret-consumption paths is not validated)
crash_site: with kubernetes.io/enforce-mountable-secrets set on a ServiceAccount, a pod that should be rejected for referencing a non-allowed Secret is admitted anyway; the admission plugin returns no error.

ground_truth.decoy_fn: the present, correct loops over volumes, container env[].valueFrom.secretKeyRef, and imagePullSecrets. The defect is the ABSENT loop over container.EnvFrom[].SecretRef (also missing in limitEphemeralContainerSecretReferences).
ground_truth.fix_summary: limitSecretReferences enforced the mountable-secrets allowlist for volumes, env valueFrom.secretKeyRef, and imagePullSecrets, but never checked container.EnvFrom[].SecretRef. A pod could reference a non-allowed Secret via envFrom and pass admission. Fix: add the missing envFrom.SecretRef check loop (in both limitSecretReferences and limitEphemeralContainerSecretReferences).

admission.note: Authored from PR #124322 (CVE-2024-3177) diff. No crash: the symptom is a pod that SHOULD be rejected being admitted; the cause is an ABSENT envFrom.SecretRef arm in limitSecretReferences (other arms present + correct). C2 STRONG: grep 'envFrom' in the buggy plugin returns nothing (missing code). FAMOUS CVE (2024-3177) -> CHECK CONTAMINATION; down-weight if baseline FULL-solves from prose. Pure Go. Run baseline first.

provenance: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/124322 (CVE-2024-3177) — limit secret refs from envFrom in ServiceAccount admission; fix b722d017a34b, base cae35dba5a30.
